source.title: How to build a cognitive map: insights from models of the hippocampal formation

confidence_rationale: This review describes how hippocampal cognitive maps compose spatial and object representations from separate streams, with landmark cells binding what and where. Supports SD-015's claim that object identity must be encoded separately from spatial context for goal-directed navigation. Confidence moderated by the review being about biological hippocampus rather than computational agent architectures.
failure_signatures: Hippocampal landmark cells bind object identity WITH spatial location (conjunctive coding), suggesting the brain may not keep what/where fully separate at the navigation level.
The review notes that 65.8% of hippocampal units show conjunctive space-landmark coding, while only 6.4% are pure landmark cells -- the majority are mixed, not separated.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The hippocampal formation builds cognitive maps by composing representations from separate streams: spatial structure (grid cells, place cells) and sensory/object identity (landmark cells). Goal-directed navigation uses these maps by computing goal vectors relative to compositional landmark-place representations.
mapping.ree_translation: SD-015's separation of z_resource (object identity) from z_world (spatial scene) parallels the compositional architecture described in this review. The dedicated ResourceEncoder extracts the 'what-to-seek' signal that E3's trajectory scoring needs, analogous to how hippocampal landmark cells encode object identity for goal-directed navigation planning.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The review shows that hippocampal representations are predominantly conjunctive (binding what and where), not purely separated. This suggests that while separate input streams are needed (supporting SD-015), the downstream navigation system may need to re-bind them. REE's E3 trajectory scorer likely needs both z_resource and z_world jointly, not z_resource alone.
mapping.source_context: Review of computational models of hippocampal formation, covering Tolman-Eichenbaum Machine, Clone-Structured Causal Graphs, and successor representation models

confidence_components.notes: Nature Neuroscience review by leading group (Behrens lab). Mapping fidelity moderate: the paper addresses cognitive map construction for navigation (directly relevant) but the specific finding about conjunctive coding partially complicates SD-015's clean separation claim. Transfer risk moderate: biological hippocampal models vs REE's E3 trajectory scoring.
